course_title: Freshman Seminar:Art History: The Arch of Druid Hills
subject: ARTHIST
course_description: Limited to freshmen and introductory in nature, these seminars may feature discussion, readings, museum visits, and presentations.
course_notes: (Freshmen Seminar - Limited to First Year Students Only) Druid Hills, adjacent to Emory University, is one of the most beautiful neighborhoods in Atlanta. Its rolling hills, linear parks, and curving roadways are lined with dogwood and azalea and a succession of graceful houses built by celebrated architects for citizens such as Asa Griggs Candler, founder of Coca-Cola. Laid out in the 1920s, under the direction of landscape architect Frederick Law Olmstead, of Central Park fame, Druid Hills developed following Candler's 1916 gift of land to build an Atlanta campus for Emory University. In this seminar, we will explore the neighborhood--both in the classroom and on foot, to understand the design of individual houses (Callanwolde, Briarcliff Mansion, Rainbow Terrace, Lullwater House), and their architects (Henry Hornbostel, Philip Trammel Schutze, Neel Reid, and Leila Ross Wilburn), the progression of landscape parks and gardens, the evolution of the urban plan.
